home.tagline: The science of eating well, living longer.
home.hero_heading: Plan your week. Eat for a longer life.
home.hero_description: Pick a meal type, tell us how many people, and get a recipe plan built from science-backed ingredients.
home.cta_ingredients_description: {count} ingredients — each one backed by research

ingredients.no_results: No ingredients found. Try a different filter or search term.
ingredients.page_description: Ingredients studied for their effect on healthy aging. Filter by category or sort A–Z.

recipes.no_results: No recipes found. Try a different search term.
recipes.page_description: Mediterranean and longevity cuisine. Every recipe is built around ingredients with proven health benefits.

sources.title: Sources
sources.description: Every claim in this wiki is grounded in peer-reviewed research. These are the books and publications we have parsed, verified, and structured into the ingredient database.
sources.coming_soon_description: We are actively parsing additional longevity research including works by Valter Longo, Peter Attia, and Dan Buettner.

support.title: Support the Wiki
support.pledge_body: Longevity Wiki will never run advertising. No sponsored content. No affiliate links disguised as recommendations. No paywalls. No data collection.
support.pledge_sub: Every ingredient article and recipe is written based on peer-reviewed science, not whoever pays the most. This is how nutrition information should work.
support.books_description: Each longevity research book we parse becomes 50-100 new ingredient articles and dozens of science-backed recipes.
support.hosting_description: Domain registration, hosting, and the occasional coffee for the person maintaining this.
support.research_description: Cross-referencing claims with PubMed studies, verifying mechanisms, updating articles when new research comes out.
support.bmc_description: One-time or monthly. Credit card, Apple Pay, Google Pay. No account needed.
support.crypto_description: Send ETH or any EVM-compatible token to this address.
support.crypto_networks: Ethereum, Polygon, Arbitrum, Optimism, Base, or any EVM chain.
support.contribute_description: This is an open source project. You can help by improving the ingredient extraction skills, adding new data, fixing bugs, or writing better wiki articles. Every pull request makes the wiki better for everyone.
support.wiki_repo_description: The website, articles, and recipes. Add ingredients, fix content, improve the design.
support.skills_repo_description: The Claude Code skills that extract, research, and generate content. Improve the pipeline, add new book parsers.
support.roadmap_sub: Where your donations take us next.
support.roadmap_illustrations_body: Every ingredient article gets a pen-and-ink SVG in the field-guide style. No stock photography, ever.
support.roadmap_books_body: Each longevity book we parse becomes 50+ new ingredient articles and dozens of science-backed recipes. Next on the shelf: Valter Longo's "The Longevity Diet," Peter Attia's "Outlive," Dan Buettner's "The Blue Zones Kitchen."
support.roadmap_symptoms_body: Pick how you feel — low energy, poor sleep, bloating — and get ingredient suggestions grounded in peer-reviewed science.
support.roadmap_devices_body: Sign in and keep your meal plans and tried ingredients on any device, with no ads and no data tracking.

meal_planner.page_description: Plan your week with evidence-based longevity recipes. Get a shopping list, track ingredient coverage.
meal_planner.title: Meal Planner
meal_planner.subtitle: {count} evidence-based recipes. Pick how many people and recipes, get a shopping list.
meal_planner.empty_state: Set how many people and recipes, then click Suggest recipes.
meal_planner.seo_intro: This planner builds a week of meals around the foods that show up most consistently in longevity research — Mediterranean staples, leafy greens, legumes, fatty fish, fermented dairy, and a small but reliable rotation of nuts, herbs, and whole grains. Every recipe in the database was written to land 3–5 of these ingredients in one dish, so a five-recipe week reliably covers 20+ longevity foods without you tracking it manually.
meal_planner.seo_step_1_title: Pick a meal type and count
meal_planner.seo_step_1_body: Tell the planner what kind of meals you want (breakfast, dinner, sauce, etc.) and how many recipes for the week. The planner rotates through the recipe library and avoids back-to-back repeats.
meal_planner.seo_step_2_title: Get a recipe plan and shopping list
meal_planner.seo_step_2_body: The planner generates a recipe set, then derives a single deduplicated shopping list — quantities scale to the number of people you're feeding. You can swap any recipe you don't like.
meal_planner.seo_step_3_body: Mark recipes as tried to keep them in your history. The planner tracks which longevity ingredients your week covered, so you can see gaps before they become habits.
meal_planner.seo_why_body: The strongest dietary predictors of healthspan in epidemiology aren't single nutrients — they're recurring food patterns. Olive oil, fish, legumes, vegetables, whole grains, nuts, and fermented dairy show up across every Blue Zone, the Mediterranean diet trials, and the longest-running cohort studies. Planning meals around these ingredients (rather than around macros or calorie counts) is the lowest-friction way to nudge weekly intake toward the patterns that show up in the data.

footer.description: Open source food encyclopedia grounded in peer-reviewed longevity research.
